steps.title: Identify the x-intercept location
steps.content: The x-intercept is the point where the parabola crosses the x-axis. At this point, $y = 0$.

steps.title: Read the x-coordinate from the grid
steps.content: Examining the graph, the parabola has its vertex at approximately $(3, -4)$. Moving rightward from the vertex, the parabola rises and crosses the x-axis at a single point. Reading directly from the grid, this crossing occurs at $x = 6$. The x-coordinate of the x-intercept is 6.
